sections.summary: Document title: "STANDARD FOR NAMED ANIMAL FATS."
Document code: CXS 211-1999.
States adoption and amendment history: adopted 1999 and amended in 2009, 2013, 2015, 2019, 2021 and 2023.
States that following decisions at the Forty-sixth Codex Alimentarius Commission (December 2023), Section 8 (Methods of analysis and sampling) of the standard was amended in 2023.
Specifies that the standard applies to the animal fats described in Section 2 when presented in a state for human consumption.

Defines the products covered (lard, rendered pork fat, premier jus, edible tallow) by specifying the animal source tissues and that animals must be in good health and fit for human consumption; it also permits certain processed forms (e.g., refined, stearin, hydrogenated) provided they are clearly labelled.

Defines pure rendered lard as fat rendered from fresh, clean, sound swine fatty tissues (excluding bones, detached skin, head skin, ears, tails, organs and similar materials and with minimal muscle/blood), and allows processed forms (refined lard, lard stearin, hydrogenated lard) provided they are clearly labelled.

Provides gas‑liquid chromatography (GLC) percentage ranges for fatty acid composition for lard, rendered pork fat, premier jus and tallow, and states that samples within the specified ranges comply with the standard.

Recommends that products be prepared and handled in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex texts, and that they comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Requires labelling in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with the product name conforming to Section 2; for non-retail containers the name, lot identification and manufacturer/packer must appear on the container but lot/manufacturer may be replaced by an identification mark if it is clearly identifiable with accompanying documents.
States that the product must be labelled per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and that the name of the fat shall conform to the descriptions in Section 2.

Same as Section 7: requires labelling per CXS 1-1985 with the food name conforming to Section 2, and specifies non-retail container labelling requirements including the option to use an identification mark linked to accompanying documents.
Specifies that methods of analysis and sampling in the Codex Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard shall be used to check compliance.

Provides supplementary (non‑essential) quality and composition factors and tables — noting a product that meets the essential factors but not these may still conform — and sets specific numerical limits and properties, including matter volatile at 105 °C (≤0.3%), insoluble impurities (≤0.05%), sodium soap content (nil or 0.005% depending on product), iron (1.5 mg/kg), copper (0.4 mg/kg), product-specific acid value limits (with FFA equivalents), peroxide value (up to 10 meq O2/kg fat), and chemical/physical ranges (density, refractive index, titre, saponification and iodine values, unsaponifiables) for lard, rendered pork fat, premier jus and tallow.

Lists quality characteristics: colour categories for each fat, odour and taste must be characteristic and free from foreign and rancid odours/tastes, and sets maximum levels including volatile matter at 105 °C (0.3%), insoluble impurities (0.05%), sodium soap content (nil or 0.005% depending on product), iron (1.5 mg/kg), copper (0.4 mg/kg), product-specific acid values with FFA equivalents, and a peroxide value limit of up to 10 milliequivalents active oxygen/kg fat.
Heading only for colour specifications (no substantive provisions in this item).
Subheading only for rendered pork fat colour (no substantive content in this item).
Subheading only for lard colour (no substantive content in this item).
Subheading only for premier jus colour (no substantive content in this item).
Subheading only for edible tallow colour (no substantive content in this item).
Heading only for odour and taste (no substantive provisions in this item).

sections.section: characteristic and free from foreign and rancid odour and taste.
sections.summary: Requires that odour and taste be characteristic and free from foreign and rancid odour and taste.
Heading only for maximum levels (no substantive provisions in this item).
Heading for the parameter 'matter volatile at 105 °C' with no limit value given in this section.
Specifies the limit for matter volatile at 105 °C as 0.3%.
Heading for the parameter 'insoluble impurities' with no numeric limit given in this section.
Specifies the limit for insoluble impurities as 0.05%.
Heading for sodium soap content limits by commodity; no values provided in this section.
Table entry naming the commodity 'lard' under sodium soap content; no limit value appears in this section.
Value 'nil' presented as a sodium soap content limit, with no commodity identified within this section.
Table entry naming the commodity 'premier jus' under sodium soap content; no limit value appears in this section.
Value 'nil' presented as a sodium soap content limit, with no commodity identified within this section.
Table entry naming the commodity 'rendered pork fat' under sodium soap content; no limit value appears in this section.
Specifies a sodium soap content limit of 0.005% (commodity not stated in this section).
Table entry naming the commodity 'edible tallow' under sodium soap content; no limit value appears in this section.
Specifies a sodium soap content limit of 0.005% (commodity not stated in this section).
Heading for the metal parameter 'Iron (Fe)' with no numeric limit given in this section.
Specifies the limit for iron (Fe) as 1.5 mg/kg.
Heading for the metal parameter 'Copper (Cu)' with no numeric limit given in this section.
Specifies the limit for copper (Cu) as 0.4 mg/kg.
Heading for the parameter 'acid value' with no numeric limit or commodity specified in this section.
Table entry naming the commodity 'lard' in the context of acid value limits; no numeric value appears in this section.
Specifies an acid value of 1.3 mg KOH/g fat, equivalent to free fatty acids (FFA) max 0.65%.
Heading indicating the product name 'Premier jus' with no substantive regulatory content.
Acid value/FFA limit: maximum 2.0 mg KOH per g fat, equivalent to free fatty acids (FFA) max 1.00%.
Heading indicating the product type 'Rendered pork fat' with no substantive regulatory content.
Acid value/FFA limit for rendered pork fat: maximum 2.5 mg KOH per g fat, equivalent to FFA max 1.25%.
Heading indicating the product type 'Edible tallow' with no substantive regulatory content.
Acid value/FFA limit for edible tallow: maximum 2.5 mg KOH per g fat, equivalent to FFA max 1.25%.
Heading introducing peroxide value criteria (no numeric limit in this line).

sections.section: up to 10 milliequivalents active oxygen/kg fat
sections.summary: Peroxide (oxidation) limit: up to 10 milliequivalents active oxygen per kilogram of fat (≤10 meq O2/kg fat).

Section heading plus a table of chemical and physical characteristics providing physicochemical limits (relative density, refractive index, titre, saponification value, iodine value, unsaponifiable matter) and appearance descriptions for Lard, Rendered pork fat, Premier jus, and Tallow.

sections.section: |                                         | Lard         | Rendered pork fat   | Premier jus   | Tallow      |
sections.summary: Table header row listing the product columns: Lard, Rendered pork fat, Premier jus, and Tallow (no numeric limits in this line).
Table formatting/separator row (no substantive regulatory content).
Relative density (40°C, water at 20°C) ranges: Lard 0.896–0.904; Rendered pork fat 0.894–0.906; Premier jus 0.893–0.904; Tallow 0.894–0.904.
Refractive index (nD 40°C) ranges: Lard 1.448–1.460; Rendered pork fat 1.448–1.461; Premier jus 1.448–1.460; Tallow 1.448–1.460.
Titre (°C) / melting point ranges: Lard 32–45°C; Rendered pork fat 32–45°C; Premier jus 42.5–47°C; Tallow 40–49°C.
Saponification value (mg KOH/g fat) ranges: Lard 192–203; Rendered pork fat 192–203; Premier jus 190–200; Tallow 190–202.
Iodine value (Wijs) ranges: Lard 55–65; Rendered pork fat 60–72; Premier jus 36–47; Tallow 40–53.
Unsaponifiable matter limits (g/kg): ≤10 for Lard and Premier jus, ≤12 for Rendered pork fat and Tallow.
Appearance description for Lard: 'white when solid'.
Appearance description for Rendered pork fat: 'white to cream'.
Appearance description for Premier jus: 'creamy white to pale yellow'.
Describes the product appearance colour as off white to pale yellow.
Appendix heading only; no substantive provisions included.
Specifies that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard.
